original: First recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Ludger becomes the first Bishop of Münster in the Holy Roman Empire
Boudouin IV with the Beard becomes earl of Flanders
Zen teacher Ketsugan performs exorcisms to free Aizōji Temple in Fukushima, Japan
Prince Louis of Bourbon elected Bishop of Liege
Duke Sigismund of Tirol ends contacts with Switzerland
Thomas Cranmer becomes Archbishop of Canterbury
Battle at Mellifont: English army under Lord Mountjoy beats Irish
Congress combines East & West Florida into Florida Territory
Ether used as an anaesthetic for 1st time by Dr Crawford Long (Georgia)
Pencil with attached eraser patented (Hyman L Lipman of Philadelphia)
Danish prince Wilhelm Georg of Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg-Glucksburg chosen as king George of Greece
Skirmish at Mount Elba, Arkansas
Florida territorial government established
Texas becomes last confederate state readmitted to Union
The 15th Amendment to the US Constitution is adopted, guaranteeing the right to vote regardless of race
The Battle for Kushka triggers the Pandjeh Incident which nearly gives rise to war between the British Empire and Russian Empire
Thomas F. Bayard becomes 1st US ambassador in Great Britain
British inventor Birt Acres films Oxford and Cambridge boat race
Dutch 2nd Chamber accepts Compulsory education law
New York's Queensboro Bridge opens, linking Manhattan & Queens
Mississippi Legislature founded The University of Southern Mississippi
Lötschberg tunnel in Switzerland (13,735 m) completed
French protectorate in Morocco established
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Canadiens (NHA) beat Portland Rosebuds (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-2 series victory
Stanley Cup, Mutual Street Arena, Toronto, ON: Toronto Arenas (NHL) beat Vancouver Millionaires (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-2 series victory; first series contested by the new NHL
Belgian Army occupies Düsseldorf
Paul Claudel's "Tête d'Or" premieres in Paris
KGY-AM in Olympia WA begins radio transmissions
WWL-AM in New Orleans LA begins radio transmissions
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority formed at Howard U in 1920 incorporates

Babberich-H soccer team forms
Newfoundland changes time to 3½ hrs W of Greenwich, repeats 44 sec
Batman debuts in the 27th issue of Detective Comics by DC Comics
First flight of the Australian C.A.C. CA-16 Wirraway military aircraft
Heinkel He 100 fighter sets a world airspeed record of 463 mph
German counter offensive in North Africa
1st RSHA-transport from France arrives in camp Birkenau
SS murders 200 inmates of Trawniki concentration camp
British 1st army recaptures Sejenane
781 British bombers attack Nuremberg
289 anti-fascists murdered by Nazis in Rombergpark Dortmund
USSR invades Austria
World War II: a defecting German pilot delivers a Messerschmitt Me 262A-1 to Americans
Australia beats NZ in cricket at 3 30pm on 2nd day
Last Test Cricket appearance of Bill O'Reilly (5-14 & 3-19)
Riot breaks out in Austurvöllur square in Reykjavík, when Iceland joins NATO
Bell Telephone Laboratories announces invention of the phototransistor in Murray Hill, New Jersey
6th Tony Awards: "The Fourposter" (play) and "The King & I" (musical) win
USSR performs nuclear test
WNED TV channel 17 in Buffalo, NY (PBS) begins broadcasting
The Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs is signed at New York
France performs underground nuclear test at Ecker Algeria
Vietnam War: A car bomb explodes in front of the U.S. Embassy in Saigon, killing 22 and wounding 183 others
General Ludvik Svoboda elected president of Czechoslovakia

Loyalists bomb water and electricity installations in Northern Ireland in the hope that the attacks would be blamed on the IRA and on elements of the civil rights movement, which was demanding an end to discrimination against Catholics
American television soap opera "Another World in Somerset" premieres on NBC, runs until 1976
USSR wins its 8th straight Ice Hockey World Championship, in Stockholm, Sweden; 10th overall win
North Vietnam launches a major conventional offensive against South Vietnam
Northern Ireland's government and parliament are dissolved by the British government, and direct rule from Westminster is introduced
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Ellsworth Bunker resigns as US ambassador to South Vietnam
James Rupert kills 11 members of his family on Easter Sunday in Hamilton, Ohio
Ron Lalonde scores the 1st hat trick by a Washington Capital
Land Day, six unarmed Palestinians killed, and hundreds injured at protest against Israel's land confiscation
Mark Medoff's "Children of a Lesser God" premieres in NYC
"Chariots of Fire" directed by Hugh Hudson and starring Ben Cross and Ian Charleson premieres at a Royal Command Film Performance (Best Picture 1982)
3rd NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Columbia 3 lands at White Sands, New Mexico
John Pielmeier's play "Agnes of God", about a nun who gives birth and insists it was immaculate conception, premieres at the Music Box Theatre, NYC; runs for 599 performances
New York Mercantile Exchange (NYMEX) begins trading in crude oil future
Ray Cooney's "Run for your Wives" premieres in London
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
US ends participation in multinational Lebanon peace force
World's most valuable tip - New York police detective Robert Cunningham offers waitress Phyllis Penzo half of a $1 lottery ticket; the next day, they win $6 million
Tamil Nadu beat Railways by inns & 144 to win Ranji Trophy
1st exhibition baseball games at Joe Robbie Stadium (Yanks-Orioles)
Last issue of Dutch Newspaper "Vr˜e Folk" (Free People)
Northern Michigan wins its 1st NCAA hockey title
PBA National Championship Won by Mike Miller
CBS TV premieres overnight news program "Up To The Minute"
P J Patterson, resigns as 6th Prime Minister of Jamaica
WNSR-FM (105.1) changes callsign to WMXV-FM (NYC)
Jamaican premier Percival Patterson wins parliamentary election
Punjab beat Maharashtra by 120 runs to win Cricket's Ranji Trophy
ABC Masters Tournament won by Hobo Boothe
Bombay beat Bengal by 8 wickets to win Cricket's Ranji Trophy
England Cricket all out for 46 at Port-of-Spain
NY Mets beats NY Yankees 5-3 in an exhibition game
TV network Channel 5 begins broadcasting in the UK
Craig Kilborn becomes the host of "The Late Late Show" on CBS; hosts the program through 2004
Marcos Pontes is the first Brazilian astronaut in space
UK Terrorism Act 2006 becomes law.
Global credit information group Experian report that economic recovery in Wales slower than the rest of the UK, forecasting growth of 1.6% vs 2.2% for the rest of the UK
American Mega Millions lottery hits a world record lottery amount of 640 million dollars
Mastercard and Visa announce a massive breach in security with over ten million compromised credit card numbers
Spanish Government cuts 27 billion Euros from its budget in a tough austerity measure
North Korea declares it is at a state of war with South Korea
North Carolina repeals its controversial bathroom law that restricted transgender use
Venezuela Supreme Court takes over legislative powers of the National Assembly, opposition calls it a coup

Palestinians begin a "Great March of Return", six weeks of protests on the Gaza Strip demanding Palestinian refugees be allowed to return to Israel. 19 Palestinians killed, 1,416 injured on the first day
Zuzana Caputova elected President of Slovakia, the country's first female head of state
International Olympic Committee announces postponed 2020 Summer Olympic Games will be held July 23-August 8 in 2021 because of the COVID-19 pandemic
Russian city Moscow begins a city-wide lockdown after 4 hours notice due to COVID-19
Three out of four Americans now ordered to stay home due to COVID-19 as states of Virginia, Maryland, Arizona and Florida issue lockdowns
Computer's Turing Award won by American programmer Jack Dongarra, whose work paved the way for supercomputers
Edward Isacks Berwick-upon-Tweed, during armed conflict between Scotland and England
PlaywrightVoltairecrowned with laurel wreath
Carl Friedrich Gauss, German mathematician, discovers the construction of the heptadecagon
Joachim Muratissues the Rimini Declaration, which later inspires Italian unification
Napoleonic Wars: Sixth Coalition forces march into Paris after defeatingNapoleon
Bedřich Smetana's comic opera "Verkaufte Braut"/ "The Bartered Bride" premieres at Prague Provisional Theatre in Prague, Kingdom of Bohemia

Gandhiannounces resistance against Rowlatt Act
1st performance ofWalter Piston's 4th Symphony commissioned to mark the University of Minnesota's centennial, debuts by the Minneapolis Symphony Orchestra, Antal Doráti conducting

27th Academy Awards: "On the Waterfront",Marlon Brando&Grace Kellywin
Dalai Lamaflees China and is granted political asylum in India
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 169,600' (51,690 m)
AstronautJohn Glennwithdraws from Ohio senate race
"Color Me Barbra",Barbra Streisand's second TV special and the first in color, premieres on CBS
Columbia Records releases jazz artistMiles Davis's influential double album "Bitches Brew"; it becomes his highest-charting title, wins a Grammy, and earns him his first gold record
59th Academy Awards: "Platoon",Paul Newman&Marlee Matlinwin
Vincent van Gogh's "Sunflowers" sells for a record £22.5 million ($39.7 million)
Jack Nicklausdebuts on the Senior PGA Tour with a 71 (-1) in the first round of The Tradition at Desert Mountain and wins the event by four strokes overGary Player
64th Academy Awards: "The Silence of the Lambs",Anthony Hopkins&Jodie Fosterwin
Man accidentally backs into Oakland A's JoseCanseco's $225,000 Lamborghini
Prince Edward& girl-friend Sophie Rhys-Jones visit Greystoke Castle
Richard Bransonis knighted byCharles, Prince of Walesfor "services to entrepreneurship" at Buckingham Palace, London
At 15 years, 9 months American swimmerMichael Phelpsbreaks 200m butterfly world record at the US World Championship trials at Austin, Texas; becomes youngest male to set a world mark
Kenya's Supreme Court declaresUhuru Kenyattathe rightful winner of the Kenyan presidential election
Ex-South Korean presidentPark Geun-hyearrested in corruption investigation
Country singer songwriterKacey Musgravesreleases her fourth album "Golden Hour" (Grammy Album of the Year 2019)
Pope Francisarrives in Rabat, Morocco, on his first-ever visit to the Magreb region of Northern Africa
Swedish gamer and YouTuberPewDiePieovertaken by Indian channel T-Series as YouTube channel with most subscribers (both on 92 million)
Bruce Willisannounces he is stepping away from acting after a diagnosis of aphasia
Rock bandFoo Fighterscancel their world tour after the unexpected death of their drummer Taylor Hawkins in Bogotá, Colombia
Former US PresidentDonald Trumpis indicted by a Manhattan Grand Jury on charges over hush payments paid to porn star Stormy Daniels - 1st US President to face criminal charges

Key figures in Artificial Intelligence includingElon MuskandSteve Wozniaksign an open letter warning the race to develop AI systems is out of control and asking for a suspension of at least six months

The Russian Empire, the Ottoman Empire, Great Britain, France and the Kingdom of Sardinia sign the Treaty of Paris ending the Crimean War
The United States buys Alaska from Russia for $7,200,000 (109 million in 2018 dollars), roughly 2 cents an acre
Cover picture of Beatles' "Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" album is photographed by Michael Cooper
Airey Neave, a British politician, is killed by a car bomb as he exits the Palace of Westminster. The Irish National Liberation Army claims responsibility.
